title: Identification of antibiotic synergistic targets
abstract: A method of identifying genes, or proteins encoded by said genes, in a microbe that contribute or influence antimicrobial sensitivity, including the steps of; producing a transposon mutant library with sequencing of all transposonâchromosome insertion junction sites in parallel, identifying primary target genes or gene products within the mutant library that contribute to microbe sensitivity to a first antimicrobial compound by subjecting said mutants to growth or survival under a number of test conditions, and wherein secondary target genes or gene products are identified from the library or primary target genes or gene products, that are sensitive to at least one second compound.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A method of identifying genes, or proteins encoded by said genes, in a microbe that contribute or influence antimicrobial sensitivity, said method including the steps of; - production of a transposon mutant library with sequencing of all transposonâ chromosome insertion junction sites in parallel, - identifying primary target genes or gene products within the mutant library that contribute to microbe sensitivity to a first antimicrobial compound by subjecting said mutants to growth or survival under a number of test conditions, characterised in that secondary target genes or gene products are identified from the library or primary target genes or gene products, that are sensitive to at least one second compound. 2. A method according to claim 1 characterised in that the transposon includes an inducible promoter. 3. A method according to claim 1 characterised in that the transposon includes an outward-directed promoter. 4. A method according to claim 1 characterised in that the second compounds are inhibitors of the protein or enzyme coded by the secondary gene targets. 5. A method according to claim 4 characterised in that the at least one second compound is a direct inhibitor. 6. A method according to claim 1 characterised in that secondary target genes or gene products are selected that provide synergy or a synergistic antimicrobial effect between the first antimicrobial compound and the at least one second compound. 7. A method according to claim 3 characterised in that the use of a transposon with an outward-directed inducible promoter allows the impact of gene overexpression and gene silencing of every gene in the microbe assayed. 8. A method according to claim 1 characterised in that transposon directed insertion-site sequencing (TraDIS) is used to produce the transposon mutant library. 9. A method according to claim 8 characterised in that that the transposon cassette includes an inducible promoter into the transposon cassette ( TraDIS-Xpress). 10. A method according to claim 9 characterised in that TraDIS-Xpress technology performs the mutant library construction in high throughput, screens the mutants and identifies putative targets for synergy. 11. A method according to claim 10 characterised in that TraDIS-Xpress assay the genome at a resolution of substantially 1-5 base pairs. 12. A method according to claim 10 characterised in that TraDIS-Xpress is used to assay essential genes by knock-down of expression. 13. A method according to any preceding claim characterised in that the transposon used to construct the mutant library is a Tn5-derivative. 14. A method according to claim 13 characterised in that transposon is a mini Tn5- derivative. 15. A method according to any preceding claim characterised in that the transposon includes an aph(3')-Ia gene. 16. A method according to claim 15 characterised in that the gene codes for kanamycin resistance. 17. A method according to claim 16 characterised in that the gene incorporates an outward-transcribing IPTG-inducible tac promoter. 18. A method according to any of claims 15-17 characterised in that the mutations are by insertional inactivation and by altered expression of adjacent genes. 19. A method according to any preceding claim characterised in that the transposon mutants are collected and stored as a single pool. 20. A method according to any preceding claim characterised in that the transposon mutant library is grown in broth supplemented with antibiotics. 21. A method according to claim 20 characterised in that following growth genomic DNA is extracted from the bacteria and fragmented. 22. A method according to claim 21 characterised in that nucleotide sequence reads are generated from the transposon promoter ends.
